timgutilities.textio.TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE

    Method
    Description

    TextIOUtils.getDouble(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, double lower, double upper)
    see getInt(prompt, type, lower, upper) but returns a double
    static double
    TextIOUtils.getDouble(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, double lower, double upper, boolean useDefault, double defaultValue)
    see getIntBase(prompt, type, lower, upper, useDefault, useDefault, defaultValue) but returns a double
    static double
    TextIOUtils.getDouble(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, double lower, double upper, double defaultValue)
    see getInt(prompt, type, lower, upper, defaultValue) but returns a double
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getInt(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper)
    Prompt the user to enter a base 10 integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the user is prompted to have another go
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getInt(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, boolean useDefault, int defaultValue)
    Prompt the user to enter a base 10 integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the default value is used.
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getInt(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, boolean useDefault, int defaultValue, int base)
    Prompt the user to enter an arbitrary base integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the default value is used.
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getInt(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, int defaultValue)
    Prompt the user to enter a base 10 integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the default value is used
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getIntBase(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, int base)
    Prompt the user to enter an arbitrary base integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the user is prompted to have another go
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getIntBase(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, int defaultValue, int base)
    Prompt the user to enter an arbitrary base integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the default value is used
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getIntHex(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper)
    Prompt the user to enter a base 16 (HEX) integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the user is prompted to have another go
    static int
    TextIOUtils.getIntHex(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, int defaultValue)
    Prompt the user to enter a base 16 (HEX) integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the default value is used
    static long

    see getInt(prompt, type, lower, upper) but returns a Long
    static long

    see getIntBase(prompt, type, lower, upper, useDefault, useDefault, defaultValue) but returns a Long
